title: Read-only diagnostic workflow for unit-talk-api-1 restart-history investigation

sha_binding.verified_source_note: New file only: .github/workflows/ops-container-diagnose.yml. Strictly read-only -- no restart, deploy, or env mutation. Mirrors the existing ops-network-diagnose.yml (UTV2-1560) and ops-api-diagnose.yml patterns already merged to main; reuses their existing UNIT_TALK_DEPLOY_HOST/USER/PATH/SSH_KEY secrets, no new secret introduced. Hardened per PM_VERDICT: CHANGES_REQUIRED: docker events now uses a generated Unix timestamp instead of the unsupported 'now' literal, and both docker-events collection and journalctl/dmesg calls now distinguish an actual command failure (permission denied, unsupported flag) from a genuine zero-matches result.

verifier.note: Mechanical checks only. Requires t1-approved label and a Griff-authored pm-verdict/v1 APPROVED comment bound to the exact head before merge.

r_level.command: pnpm exec tsx scripts/ci/r-level-check.ts --base origin/main --head HEAD

runtime_proof.note: This lane adds only a new read-only CI workflow file; no production code path or migration touched. Live smoke run regardless per T1 policy.

acceptance_criteria.captures_image_deployed_sha: PASS -- docker inspect .Image/.Config.Image + /health endpoint version block
acceptance_criteria.captures_docker_daemon_events: PASS -- docker events --since <generated-ts> --until <generated-ts>, container-scoped and die/oom/restart/health_status-scoped
acceptance_criteria.captures_kernel_oom_events: PASS -- journalctl -k grep oom, dmesg fallback, each with an explicit command-failure marker distinct from zero matches
acceptance_criteria.captures_docker_daemon_service_log: PASS -- journalctl -u docker, with an explicit command-failure marker distinct from an empty log
acceptance_criteria.captures_deploy_correlation_evidence: PASS -- .env.production mtime; deploy-timestamp correlation itself is done at the analysis layer against gh run history for deploy.yml, not inside the SSH probe
acceptance_criteria.no_api_restart_deploy_or_env_mutation: PASS -- every command is a read-only inspect/logs/events/journalctl/df/free/stats call
acceptance_criteria.docker_events_uses_supported_until_value: PASS -- replaced the unsupported 'now' literal with a generated Unix timestamp (date +%s), per Docker CLI docs
acceptance_criteria.docker_events_collection_errors_are_visi: PASS -- each docker events call captures its own exit code and prints an explicit FAILED marker (not silently blended into a tail of whatever error text came back) if it errors
acceptance_criteria.journalctl_dmesg_permission_failures_dis: PASS -- journalctl -k, dmesg, and journalctl -u docker each capture their own exit code before grepping, so a permission failure prints a distinct FAILED marker instead of looking identical to a genuine zero-matches result

codex_review_fixes.finding: docker events --until now is not a Docker-documented value (accepted forms are Unix timestamps, formatted timestamps, or Go duration strings); on hosts where the CLI rejects 'now', the command errors and set +e lets the step pass with only an error string in the output, silently missing all restart/oom/health events
codex_review_fixes.fix: Both docker events calls now use a generated Unix timestamp pair ($(date +%s) and now-604800) instead of the literal 'now'

codex_review_fixes.finding: Both docker events calls and the journalctl/dmesg calls piped output through grep/tail and relied on `|| echo '...unavailable or no matches'`, which cannot distinguish a genuine empty result from the command itself failing (e.g. permission denied) -- exactly the ambiguity the proof's own known_gaps warned about
codex_review_fixes.fix: Each of the five collection commands (2x docker events, journalctl -k, dmesg, journalctl -u docker) now captures its own exit code via command substitution before any grep/tail, and prints an explicit '... FAILED (exit N)' marker distinct from a 'succeeded, zero matches' message

known_gaps: docker events covers only a 7-day window; if the 314-restart count accumulated over a longer period, older events will already have rotated out of the daemon's event log and won't appear here -- the RestartCount/State fields still give the current lifetime total regardless.

journalctl/dmesg access still depends on the deploy user's permissions on the host; this is now visible as an explicit FAILED marker rather than being silently indistinguishable from a real zero-matches result, per the fix above.
